== System Overview

=== Core Trust Challenge

Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.

This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:

*
{st_container_s}
provide powerful tool isolation
* But they need controlled access to do useful work
* Standard
{st_container}
networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.

=== The Bottle Pattern

{at_rbm_system}
solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
{st_container}
type here referred to as a
{at_bottle_container}:

. File Access Boundary
*
{at_bottle_container}
sees only explicitly controlled set of
{st_volume_mount_s}
* No other access to
{at_workstation}
filesystem

. Network Boundary
*
{at_bottle_container}
has no direct network access
* All traffic flows through dedicated
{at_sentry_container},
a
{st_container}
configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
* Only declared
{st_port_map_s}
and destinations allowed
* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic

Each
{rbsc_crucible}
maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:

* One
{at_sentry_container}
enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
* One
{at_pentacle_container}
establishing and configuring the network namespace
* One
{at_bottle_container}
running the actual service, sharing the pentacle's network namespace via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`
* Clear configuration in a single
{at_nameplate}
file, extended by per-nameplate compose fragments for volume mounts

Docker Compose orchestrates the container lifecycle declaratively.
A base compose file (`rbob_compose.yml`) defines the three-container topology with health check chains: the
{at_sentry_container}
must be healthy before the
{at_pentacle_container}
starts, and the
{at_pentacle_container}
must be healthy before the
{at_bottle_container}
starts.
This prevents firewall bypass — the bottle cannot send traffic before security policies are enforced.
Per-nameplate compose fragments (e.g., `«moniker».compose.yml`) add volume mount declarations.
Env-files passed via `--env-file` serve a dual purpose: YAML interpolation (`${VAR}` references in compose files resolve image refs, subnet, container names) and container environment forwarding (bare variable names in `environment:` sections inject values into containers at runtime).

The
{at_pentacle_container}
is a privileged container that configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
{at_sentry_container},
then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
{at_bottle_container}
via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`.
This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
{at_bottle_container}
experiences only a functional path to its
{at_sentry_container}
gateway

=== Security Properties

The
{at_rbm_system}
maintains following essential guarantees:

. File Access Control
*
{at_bottle_container_s}
can access only explicitly configured
{st_volume_mount_s}
* No access to broader system resources

. Network Isolation
* All traffic flows through dedicated
{at_sentry_container}
* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
* Only explicitly configured
{st_port_map_s}
allow traffic between
{at_transit_network}
and
{at_enclave_network}
*
{at_bottle_container_s}
have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
{at_sentry_container_s}
* Comprehensive traffic logging

. Service Separation
* Each
{rbsc_crucible}
fully isolated
* No cross-service communication
* Independent security policies
* Clean state separation

. Service Access Control
* External access to services only permitted when
{rbrn_entry_mode}
is enabled
* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
* All service access must originate from outside the pod
* Entry traffic flows: external -> pod ->
{at_sentry_container}
->
{at_bottle_container}

These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.

== Google API Engagement

{at_recipe_bottle}
invokes
{gcp_service}
APIs through
{giam_service_account}
JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.

* *Authentication*:
{giam_service_account_s}
use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
These JWTs are exchanged for
{oauth_token_s}
via Google's token endpoint.
All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.

* *Invocation Pattern*:
Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.

* *Long-Running Operations*:
{gcp_service}
{gcp_lro_s}
return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
The system handles both success and error states.
Operations utilize
{rbbc_submit}
and
{rbbc_await}
to handle all asynchronous operations.

* *IAM Policies*:
All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
{gcp_service}
resources.

* *IAM Member Format*:
All IAM member bindings use `serviceAccount:«EMAIL»` format.
+
GCP IAM policy `members` fields only accept email-format identifiers (`serviceAccount:name@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).
The numeric `uniqueId` returned by `serviceAccounts.create` is valid for *API URL paths* (e.g., `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«UNIQUE_ID»` for GET/verification) but *not* for policy binding members.
`serviceAccount:«NUMERIC_ID»` returns HTTP 400 "Invalid service account" on all setIamPolicy endpoints — project, repository, and bucket alike.
+
Google's documentation recommends numeric IDs "to identify a service account just after it is created" (link:https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/manage-access-service-accounts[Manage access to service accounts]), but this applies to API resource addressing, not to IAM policy member strings.
The internal IAM system resolves email→numeric transparently; callers must use email.
+
Google-provisioned service agents (Cloud Build) are not affected — they are managed by Google, not newly created.

== Operational Invariants

=== Concurrent Session Safety

{at_recipe_bottle}
commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.

Concurrency safety is achieved through:

* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BURD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.

* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
Cloud resources (build IDs, inscribe timestamps) incorporate unique identifiers, preventing overwrite conflicts in
{rbtgi_repository}.

* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.

* *Semantic Preconditions*:
Operations like
{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
require clean git state.
This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.

NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.

NOTE: The
{gcb_service}
private pool enforces a *Concurrent Build CPUs (Private Pool)* quota per project per region.
Machine type is set at pool creation time via
{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
(a Compute Engine type such as `e2-standard-2`); individual build requests do not specify machine type.
If the pool's vCPU allocation is nearly exhausted, a second
build invocation will remain in `QUEUED` status while the first is `WORKING`.
The
{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
See
{rbtgo_quota_build}
for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.

== Key Premises

[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
{at_recipe_bottle}
scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
All local
{gcp_service}
interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
+
**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).

== Dependency Inventory

{at_recipe_bottle} depends on a minimal, explicitly managed set of external commands.
The Bash Console Guide (`BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) establishes the universal dependency discipline — POSIX builtins preferred, external commands justified individually.
This section inventories the concrete choices {at_recipe_bottle} has made within that framework.

=== Consumer Dependencies

Required on every {at_workstation}.
Enforced by qualification and documented on the project page.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `bash`
| Shell runtime (3.2+ compatible)

| `git`
| Version control and subtree integration

| `curl`
| HTTP client for all GCP REST API interaction

| `jq`
| JSON parsing for API responses and configuration

| `docker`
| {at_runtime} for bottle lifecycle

| `openssl`
| JWT signing (RS256), base64 encoding/decoding, SHA-256 hashing

| `ssh` / `scp` / `ssh-keygen`
| Secure credential transport and key generation
|===

=== Developer Dependencies

Used in development and testing tooling.
Not required for consumer workflows.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `shellcheck`
| Static analysis linter, used by release-tier qualification

| `timeout`
| GNU coreutils test timeout, used by testbench; absent on stock macOS
|===

=== Specialized Runtime Dependencies

Present in specific operational modules.
Not required for core consumer or developer workflows.

[cols="1,3"]
|===
| Command | Purpose

| `podman`
| Alternative container runtime (deferred: dual-runtime support alongside docker)

| `tcpdump`
| Network debugging in observe module

| `sed`
| 2 load-bearing uses: log timestamp normalization, Podman output parsing

| `stty`
| Terminal width detection in launcher
|===

include::RBSIR-image_rekon.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_abjure]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_abjure}

Formally renounces an
{rbtga_ark}
from the repository
({rbtgog_ark}),
removing its image, about, and vouch artifacts as a coherent unit.

include::RBSAA-ark_abjure.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_ordain]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_ordain}

Ordains a hallmark from a vessel — the master ceremony dispatch operation.
Detects vessel mode (conjure, bind, or graft) and delegates to the appropriate
{rbtga_ark}
creation path, then chains metadata and vouch pipeline stages.

[[rbtgo_ark_conjure]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_conjure}

Conjures an
{rbtga_ark_image}
by building a container image
({rbtgog_ark})
from a vessel specification.
Build context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}, submitted via `builds.create` API. The combined job produces both `-image` and `-about` (about steps embedded via stitch). Vouch is a separate Cloud Build job using DSSE envelope signature verification.

include::RBSAC-ark_conjure.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_enshrine]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_enshrine}

Enshrines upstream base images into
{gar_service}
({rbtgog_ark}),
producing content-addressed
{rbrv_image_anchor}
references from
{rbrv_image_origin}
declarations.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a Cloud Build job that copies each upstream image by tag to GAR with a human-readable anchor tag, and writes the anchor back to the vessel regime.

include::RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_graft]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_graft}

Grafts a locally-built container image into the
{rbtgi_repository}
({rbtgog_ark})
via native docker/podman push.
The image push itself is local (no Cloud Build); about and vouch follow as Cloud Build jobs.

include::RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_about]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_about}

Assembles the
{rbtga_ark_about}
metadata artifact for an existing
{rbtga_ark_image}
({rbtgog_ark}).
Runs Syft for SBOM generation and builds mode-aware `build_info.json` using uniform
{gcb_service}
steps.
Conjure and bind embed these steps in their primary Cloud Build job alongside image production; graft's primary job consists of these steps alone.

include::RBSAB-ark_about.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_ark_vouch]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_ark_vouch}

Vouches for an
{rbtga_ark}
({rbtgog_ark}),
producing a
{rbtga_ark_vouch}
that records mode-aware verification results.
Conjure: DSSE envelope signature verification (Python 3 + openssl against GCB attestor keys).
Bind: digest-pin comparison.
Graft: GRAFTED stamp.
Submitted as a
{gcb_service}
job via `builds.create` after
{rbtgo_ark_about}
completes.

include::RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_director_vouch]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_director_vouch}

Batch-vouches all pending
{rbtga_ark}
hallmarks across all
{rbtga_vessel}
repositories
({rbtgog_ark}).
Bind and graft hallmarks are always vouchable; conjure hallmarks require SLSA provenance pre-check.

include::RBSDV-director_vouch.adoc[]

== Term Definitions

=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms

[[at_recipe_bottle]]
{at_recipe_bottle}::
The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
{gcp_service}
infrastructure.

=== RB Role Infrastructure

[[rbtr_role]]
//axl_voices axo_identity
{rbtr_role}::
An operational identity within the
{at_recipe_bottle}
system
that an
{at_operator}
can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
Each
{rbtr_role}
is implemented through a dedicated
{giam_service_account}
and corresponding
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
{rbtr_mason}
which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.

=== {giam_service_account} Definitions

[[rbtr_payor]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_payor}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that enables an
{at_operator}
to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
The
{rbtr_payor}
operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.

[[rbtr_governor]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_governor}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that grants an
{at_operator}
{gcp_project_id}
-scope Owner permissions without billing authority, designed for automation-friendly operations.

[[rbtr_retriever]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_retriever}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that allows an
{at_operator}
read-only access to the
{rbtgi_repository}
for pulling
{rbtgi_image_s}.

[[rbtr_mason]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_mason}::
A cloud-native
{giam_service_account}
that operates exclusively within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
to execute
{at_recipe_bottle}
build instructions from the
{rbtr_director}.

The
{rbtr_mason}
impersonates the implicit service agent for builds, replacing Google's auto-created service agent with deterministic permissions and predictable authorization.

The
{rbtr_mason}
reads from the
{rbtgi_build_bucket},
carries out the build process within
{gcb_service},
and publishes
{rbtgi_image_s}
and
{rbtgi_metadata_s}
to the
{rbtgi_repository}
without requiring a local
{rbtgi_rbra_file}
since it operates entirely within the cloud infrastructure.

[[rbtr_director]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_director}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that grants an
{at_operator}
full build lifecycle permissions including Cloud Build submission and
{rbtgi_repository}
management.

=== Local Role Definitions

[[rbtr_marshal]]
//axl_voices axo_actor
{rbtr_marshal}::
An
{rbtr_role}
that qualifies and prepares releases for distribution.
Unlike other
{rbtr_role_s},
the
{rbtr_marshal}
has no
{giam_service_account}
or cloud identity — the
{rbtr_role}
taxonomy concerns operational authority, not authentication type.
Marshal operations are invisible to
{rbtr_consumer_s}.

=== {gcp_service} Definitions

[[rbtgi_depot_project]]
//axl_voices axig_project
{rbtgi_depot_project}::
The dedicated
{gcp_service}
project instance containing all Recipe Bottle depot resources, APIs, and
{giam_service_account_s}
for container building and management. Created and managed by the
{rbtr_payor}
role.

[[rbtgi_depot]]
{rbtgi_depot}::
A cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure comprising a
{rbtgi_depot_project},
{rbtgi_build_bucket},
{rbtgi_repository},
and
{rbtr_mason}
service account that share their productive lifecycle. Created as a unit by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
and destroyed as a unit by
{rbtgo_depot_unmake}.

[[rbtgi_payor_project]]
//axl_voices axig_project
{rbtgi_payor_project}::
The administrative
{gcp_service}
project that hosts the
{rbtr_payor}
{giam_service_account}.
This
{gcp_project_id}
serves as the control plane for creating and managing multiple
{rbtgi_depot_project}
instances.


[[rbtgi_build_bucket]]
//axl_voices axig_bucket
{rbtgi_build_bucket}::
The dedicated
{gcs_bucket}
within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
that holds
{at_recipe_bottle}
build artifacts with configured lifecycle policies.

=== {gar_service} Definitions

[[rbtgi_repository]]
//axl_voices axig_artifact_repository
{rbtgi_repository}::
The managed
{gar_service}
repository within the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
that stores
{at_recipe_bottle}
{rbtgi_image_s}
and metadata.

[[rbtgi_image]]
//axl_voices axig_container_image
{rbtgi_image}::
A
{at_recipe_bottle}
container image stored in the
{rbtgi_repository}
with associated tags and digest.

[[rbtgi_metadata]]
//axl_voices axig_generic_artifact
{rbtgi_metadata}::
A compressed archive containing
{at_recipe_bottle}
build transcript, SBOM, and provenance data stored in the
{rbtgi_repository}
as a Generic Artifact.
For multi-platform vessels, SBOMs and build information are per-platform — each architecture gets its own SBOM (generated by Syft scanning that platform's image) and its own `build_info.json`.
+
`build_info.json` fields are mode-aware — see {rbtga_ark_about} for the full field inventory.
Shared fields include `vessel_mode`, `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`; conjure adds SLSA provenance fields; bind adds `bind_source`; graft adds `graft_source`.

=== Ark Definitions

[[rbtga_ark]]
//axl_voices axig_artifact_group
{rbtga_ark}::
The coherent unit of a
{at_recipe_bottle}
build output: a set of artifacts in the
{rbtgi_repository}
sharing a common
{rbtga_hallmark}
but distinguished by suffix.
An ark comprises the
{rbtga_ark_image}
(`-image` suffix) containing the container image, the
{rbtga_ark_about}
(`-about` suffix) containing build metadata and SBOM, the
{rbtga_ark_vouch}
(`-vouch` suffix) containing verification results, the
{rbtga_ark_pouch}
(`-pouch` suffix) containing the build context, and the
{rbtga_ark_diags}
(`-diags` suffix) containing build diagnostics.
+
An ark reaches the repository through one of three arrival paths:
+
* *Conjure*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job builds the image (context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}) and embeds about steps (via stitch), producing both `-image` and `-about`. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verifies SLSA provenance via DSSE envelope signature verification in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Bind*: A single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job copies the pinned upstream image and runs about steps, producing both `-image` and `-about`. Then {rbtgo_ark_vouch} records a digest-pin verdict in a separate Cloud Build job.
* *Graft*: Local docker/podman push places the image as `-image`. Then a single `builds.create` {gcb_service} job runs about steps followed by vouch steps, producing both `-about` and `-vouch` in one submission.
+
Conjure and bind each use two Cloud Build round-trips (primary job + vouch job). Graft uses one (combined about+vouch job), since the image arrives via local push rather than a Cloud Build step.
Vouch is mandatory: an ark without a vouch artifact is incomplete.
Arks are abjured atomically by
{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.

[[rbtga_hallmark]]
//axl_voices axtg_timestamp
{rbtga_hallmark}::
The identifier marking when a vessel was hallmarked into an
{rbtga_ark}.
Minted on the host before submission — the host passes the hallmark to Cloud Build via the `_RBGY_HALLMARK` substitution variable.
+
Mode prefixes and formats:
+
* `c` — conjure (Cloud Build build from Dockerfile): `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`
* `b` — bind (mirror pinned upstream image): `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`
* `g` — graft (local build pushed to GAR): `g«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`
* `k` — kludge (local build for development): `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«GIT_CONTEXT»`
+
For conjure, bind, and graft: `«MODE»YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS` where the `r` prefix means "realized" — the moment the image materialized in GAR.
Regex: `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}`.
Example: `c260224153022-r260224160530` (27 characters).
+
T1 (mode-prefixed): when the operation was initiated — inscribe for conjure, mirror for bind, OCI image creation time for graft.
T2 (r-prefixed): when the image landed in GAR.
+
For graft, T1 is an intrinsic fact about the image (its `created` metadata), not an operational timestamp.
Reproducible builds may produce `T1=700101000000` — this is a true statement, not an error.
+
For kludge, the hallmark uses a git-context suffix instead of a realized timestamp, since no GAR push occurs.
Kludge hallmarks are local-only and never appear in the {rbtgi_repository}.
+
Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags:
+
[cols="3,1,5"]
|===
|Tag |Lifecycle |Purpose

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-image`
|Durable
|Consumer multiplatform manifest list (from `buildx --push`), or single-platform image

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-about`
|Durable
|SBOM + build info

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-vouch`
|Durable
|SLSA verification record

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-diags`
|Durable
|Build diagnostics

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-pouch`
|Durable
|Build context (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR)

|`«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH»`
|Durable
|Per-platform provenance-carrying images pushed via CB `images:` field

|`VOUCHES:«HALLMARK»-vouch`
|Durable
|Cross-vessel vouch index — same content as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-vouch` (GAR deduplicates blobs)
|===
+
The `-attest-«ARCH»` tags satisfy the {rbtgr_build_json} `images:` field for SLSA provenance generation.
Provenance is keyed by digest in the Container Analysis API, but the `-attest-«ARCH»` tags are the only objects carrying those digests — the classic Docker image store re-serializes manifests on pull/push, producing different digests than the buildx-native manifests in `-image`.
The tags are durable ark artifacts, persisting alongside `-image`, `-about`, `-vouch`, `-pouch`, and `-diags`.
+
Multiple conjures produce distinct hallmarks (unique realized timestamps) with independently-attested images.
+
The `VOUCHES` row in the tag table is the VOUCHES superdirectory — a single GAR package (`RBGC_VOUCHES_PACKAGE`) that aggregates all vouch artifacts across all vessels.
{rbtgo_ark_vouch} pushes each vouch artifact into both the vessel package and the VOUCHES package.
Since GAR is content-addressable, dual-tagging shares blobs — the VOUCHES tag is a pointer, not a copy.
The VOUCHES package enables
{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
to accept a hallmark without knowing its vessel: look up `VOUCHES:«HALLMARK»-vouch`, read `vouch_summary.json` to discover the vessel, then fetch about from the vessel package.

[[rbtga_ark_image]]
//axl_voices axig_container_image
{rbtga_ark_image}::
The container image artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-image` suffix.
Contains the executable container layers, regardless of how the image arrived in GAR.
+
* *Conjure*: built by {gcb_service} from a Dockerfile. SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance is auto-generated.
* *Bind*: mirrored from a pinned upstream registry reference. No SLSA provenance (upstream image, not locally built).
* *Graft*: locally built and pushed via native docker/podman push. No SLSA provenance (no Cloud Build involvement).
+
**Single-arch vessels**: a single-platform image. For conjure, pushed via the `images:` field with SLSA provenance.
+
**Multi-platform vessels** (conjure only): the consumer-facing `-image` tag is the multi-platform manifest list produced directly by `buildx --push`.
SLSA provenance is generated per platform via durable `-attest-«ARCH»` tags in the {rbtgr_build_json} `images:` field.
The manifest list itself has `slsa_build_level: unknown` (expected — Cloud Build did not build the manifest list artifact); consumers pulling the `-image` tag get transparent platform resolution.
The `-attest-«ARCH»` tags carry the only digests with GCB provenance (classic Docker image store re-serializes manifests, producing different digests than buildx-native).
Bind vessels may be multi-platform (upstream images often are; crane copy preserves the manifest list).
Graft vessels are typically single-platform.

[[rbtga_ark_about]]
//axl_voices axig_generic_artifact
{rbtga_ark_about}::
The metadata artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-about` suffix.
Contains SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generated by Syft and `build_info.json` assembled by the
{rbtgo_ark_about}
Cloud Build steps.
Created uniformly across all three vessel modes as part of a Cloud Build job: conjure and bind embed about steps in their primary image job; graft combines about and vouch steps in a single metadata job.
When a Dockerfile path is configured in the vessel regime, its content is embedded as `recipe.txt` (subject to ~4KB Cloud Build substitution variable size limit; omitted with a warning if exceeded).
+
`build_info.json` is a nested JSON object with mode-aware fields:
root — `hallmark`, `vessel_mode` (`conjure`/`bind`/`graft`), `vessel_name`, `platform`, `image_digest`, `about_timestamp`;
`git` object — `repo`, `branch`, `commit`;
conjure `build` object — `build_id`, `inscribe_timestamp`, `qemu_used`;
conjure `slsa` object — `build_level`, `build_invocation_id`, `provenance_predicate_types`, `provenance_builder_id`;
bind `bind` object — `source` (original upstream image reference);
graft `graft` object — `source` (local image reference at push time).
+
**Single-arch vessels**: a single-platform `-about` container with one SBOM and one `build_info.json`.
+
**Multi-platform vessels**: built with `buildx --push` using `FROM scratch` and Docker's automatic `TARGETARCH`/`TARGETVARIANT` build arguments to select per-platform files.
Each platform's layer contains that architecture's SBOM and `build_info.json`.
The `-about` container mirrors the `-image` container's platform list.

[[rbtga_ark_vouch]]
//axl_voices axig_verification_artifact
{rbtga_ark_vouch}::
The mandatory verification artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-vouch` suffix.
Created by
{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
as a
{gcb_service}
job submitted after
{rbtgo_ark_about}
completes.
The vouch verdict depends on vessel mode:
+
* *Conjure*: DSSE envelope signature verification of GCB-signed provenance against each per-platform digest. Records per-platform verification verdicts.
* *Bind*: Digest-pin comparison — verifies the image digest in GAR matches the pinned upstream reference. No SLSA provenance exists for upstream images.
* *Graft*: GRAFTED stamp — records that the image was locally built and pushed without provenance chain. An explicit trust boundary marker.
+
The vouch artifact is a multi-platform `FROM scratch` container holding `vouch_summary.json` and per-platform `verify-«SUFFIX».json` files (conjure) or a single `vouch_summary.json` (bind/graft).
Content is architecture-independent metadata; multi-platform is for pull ergonomics (no platform mismatch warning on arm64 hosts).
+
**Vouch depends on about**: {rbtgo_ark_vouch} gates on `-about` existence before submission.
The about pipeline must complete before vouch can run.
+
**DSSE envelope verification** (conjure mode only, ₣Av):
Provenance signatures verified against embedded GCB attestor public keys from `projects/verified-builder` KMS.
Step 1 (Alpine) writes the attestor public key to `/workspace/keys/`.
Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) fetches provenance via `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance`, extracts the v1.0 DSSE envelope, decodes base64url payload + signature, reconstructs PAE, and verifies via `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess against the embedded attestor key.
No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python `json` + `base64` modules + openssl subprocess only.
+
**Vouch Cloud Build job architecture**:
Vouch uses the RunBuild API (`builds.create`) rather than trigger dispatch — the hallmark is known only after the image arrives in GAR, so a pre-registered trigger cannot provide it.
The build runs as the {rbtr_mason} service account.
The vouch step branches on `_RBGV_VESSEL_MODE`: conjure runs DSSE provenance verification, bind runs digest-pin comparison, graft stamps GRAFTED.
+
Discovered constraints from implementation:
+
  * **Private worker pool**: The RunBuild API caller ({rbtr_director}) requires `cloudbuild.workerpools.use` to specify a private pool, a permission not included in `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor`.
    {rbtgo_director_knight} grants `roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser` at project scope.
    Vouch runs on the same private pool as conjure (`RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM`), eliminating an untested default-pool-with-buildx permutation.
  * **gcloud exception**: Vouch verification (conjure mode) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe` for provenance retrieval (see {rbsk_no_gcloud} exception).
    All other steps use the GCE metadata server for OAuth tokens and direct registry REST API calls.
  * **Builder image selection**: Step 1 (Alpine) writes attestor keys (conjure) or is a no-op (bind/graft).
    Step 2 (gcloud/Python 3) runs mode-aware verification.
    Step 3 (Docker) assembles and pushes the vouch container (`docker buildx build --push` for multi-platform).
  * **GCB dollar escaping**: Step scripts embedded in Build JSON require `$$` for literal `$` and un-escaped `${_RBGV_*}` for GCB substitution variables, following the same `sed` pattern as conjure's `${_RBGY_*}`.
  * **DSSE verification**: Provenance envelope signatures verified against embedded GCB attestor public keys (`projects/verified-builder` KMS). PAE reconstruction + `openssl dgst -sha256 -verify` subprocess. No slsa-verifier, no jq — Python 3 standard library only.
    builds.create provenance achieves SLSA Build Level 3 (₢AvAAB research confirmed).
+
**Idempotent re-vouch**:
Re-running {rbtgo_ark_vouch} on a hallmark that already has a `-vouch` artifact re-executes verification.
For conjure and bind, the new vouch content should match the existing vouch (same digests + same provenance/pin + same verifier = identical output).
For graft, the GRAFTED stamp is always identical.
A mismatch for conjure/bind indicates provenance change or tampering and must produce a loud alarm.
If determinism proves unreliable in practice, narrow the comparison to verification verdict, digests, and builder identity.
+
**Vouch provenance resolution**: The vouch verifier resolves per-platform digests from `-attest-«ARCH»` tags (HEAD request per tag) rather than from the `-image` manifest index.
The `-attest-«ARCH»` digests are the GCB-attested digests; the `-image` manifest's per-platform digests are buildx-native and carry no GCB provenance.
+
**Abjure**: {rbtgo_ark_abjure} checks for and deletes the `-vouch` artifact alongside `-image`, `-about`, `-pouch`, `-diags`, and (for conjure vessels) `-attest-«ARCH»` tags.
+
**Display**: {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} includes a Vouch column showing `yes`/`no` per hallmark, detected by matching the `-vouch` tag suffix in the GAR tag list.

[[rbtga_ark_pouch]]
//axl_voices axig_build_context
{rbtga_ark_pouch}::
The build context artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-pouch` suffix.
A FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR containing the Dockerfile, step scripts, and regime-derived configuration needed to reproduce the build.
Submitted as the `source` for `builds.create` API calls.
+
The pouch is assembled on the host by the ordain pipeline and pushed to GAR before the Cloud Build job is submitted.
Its content is deterministic given the vessel regime and git state — the same inputs produce the same pouch.
Conjure and bind modes create a pouch; graft mode does not (the image arrives via local push, not Cloud Build).

[[rbtga_ark_diags]]
//axl_voices axig_generic_artifact
{rbtga_ark_diags}::
The diagnostics artifact within an
{rbtga_ark},
tagged with the `-diags` suffix.
Contains build-time diagnostic output (step timings, builder versions, platform details) for troubleshooting.
Pushed as a dedicated step in the build pipeline.

[[rbtga_vessel]]
//axl_voices axig_build_specification
{rbtga_vessel}::
A named container image specification in the regime that defines what to build and how it arrives in the
{rbtgi_repository}.
Each vessel has a
{rbrv_vessel_mode}
(conjure, bind, or graft) that determines its arrival path, and produces
{rbtga_ark_s}
stamped with a
{rbtga_hallmark}
on each successful build.
+
A vessel's regime file (`rbrv.env`) declares its mode, Dockerfile path (conjure), upstream reference (bind), or local image (graft), along with platform targets and build configuration.
The vessel name appears in all artifact tags as the stem before the hallmark.

[[rbtgr_build_json]]
//axl_voices axig_build_config
{rbtgr_build_json}::
The generated `cloudbuild.json` for a vessel, produced from step scripts and regime pins by the stitch function.
All values resolved directly at stitch time — no placeholders, no post-processing.
Submitted via `builds.create` API. Build context delivered via {rbtga_ark_pouch}.
See <<rbscj_trade_study>>.

[[rbtgr_provenance]]
//axl_voices axig_slsa_provenance
{rbtgr_provenance}::
SLSA v1.0 build provenance attestation auto-generated by Google for `builds.create` submissions.
Records build inputs, environment, and outputs as a cryptographically signed attestation on the built image.
+
Requires two conditions in the
{rbtgr_build_json}:
(1) `options.requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` — opts private pool builds into provenance generation (private pools default to no provenance);
(2) the top-level `images:` field must list the image URI — Cloud Build generates provenance only for images it pushes via this field.
Cloud Build pre-populates Docker credentials for all GAR regions, so explicit docker-login steps are unnecessary.
+
**Single-arch vessels**: `buildx --load` places the built image into the local Docker daemon's store.
The `images:` field in {rbtgr_build_json} causes Cloud Build to push the image and generate SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance.
Verified on builds.create submissions — rbev-busybox on depot10030 (3 platforms: amd64, arm64, armv7) with DSSE envelope signature verification (₣Av ₢AvAAD).
+
**Single-arch pipeline** (3 steps + `images:` push, single build invocation):
+
  1. `derive-tag-base` — receive host-minted {rbtga_hallmark} via `_RBGY_HALLMARK`, write to `.hallmark` + `/builder/outputs/output`
  2. `qemu-binfmt` — register cross-architecture emulation (skipped if native platform)
  3. `build-and-load` — `buildx --load` into local Docker daemon (single platform)
+
About assembly (syft, build_info, metadata push) uses reusable {rbtgo_ark_about} scripts (`rbgja/`) embedded in the stitched JSON alongside the build steps.
+
**Multi-platform vessels**: production-validated architecture.
Docker's local store (classic image store on CB workers, Docker 20.10.24) is single-platform only — `buildx --load` cannot load multi-platform images.
The validated approach uses per-platform pullback after `buildx --push`:
`buildx --push` builds all platforms and pushes the consumer-facing `-image` manifest list directly to GAR;
`docker pull --platform «ARCH»` pulls each platform variant back to the local daemon (requires Experimental: true, confirmed on CB workers);
each pulled image is tagged as `«HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH»`;
the `images:` field declares all `-attest-«ARCH»` tags — Cloud Build pushes each and generates independent SLSA v1.0 Level 3 provenance with the same `buildInvocationId`, proving single-build origin.
+
The `-attest-«ARCH»` tags are **ephemeral attestation scaffolding**: they exist solely to bridge `buildx --push` output to the `images:` field for SLSA provenance generation.
Cloud Build has no awareness of the `buildx --push` to GAR — the `images:` field is CB's only mechanism for pushing and attesting images.
Provenance is keyed by digest in the Container Analysis API, not by tag.
The host sweeps `-attest-«ARCH»` tags after vouch completes using {rbtr_director} credentials (repoAdmin); the {rbtr_mason} lacks delete permission.
Deleting these tags does not delete provenance — the digests referenced by the `-image` manifest list retain their attestations.
+
Validated in experiments and production on depot `demo1025`:
per-platform provenance (`b3fd60c7`) — all 3 platforms (amd64, arm64, armv7) achieved SLSA Level 3 with shared `buildInvocationId`;
production e2e (`2e172ce0`) — build of `rbev-busybox` (3 platforms) achieved SLSA Level 3 on all per-platform images with shared `buildInvocationId`, consumer-facing manifest list resolved correctly.
The manifest list itself has `slsa_build_level: unknown` (expected — Cloud Build did not build the manifest list artifact), but consumers pulling the `-image` tag get transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform digests with full SLSA Level 3.
+
Implementation notes:
`docker pull --platform` works on CB workers (Docker 20.10.24, Experimental: true);
`$${VAR}` escaping required for shell variables in {rbtgr_build_json} (Cloud Build interprets `${VAR}` as substitution variables);
`jq` is not available in `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` — the `--platform` flag approach avoids JSON manifest parsing entirely.
See `Memos/memo-20260305-provenance-architecture-gap.md` for full experiment details.
+
**Multi-platform pipeline** (6 steps + `images:` push, single build invocation):
+
  1. `derive-tag-base` — receive host-minted {rbtga_hallmark} via `_RBGY_HALLMARK`, write to `.hallmark` + `/builder/outputs/output`
  2. `qemu-binfmt` — register cross-architecture emulation (if needed)
  3. `buildx --push` — build all platforms, push consumer-facing `-image` manifest list to GAR
  4. `per-platform-pullback` — `docker pull --platform «ARCH»` from `-image`, `docker tag` to `«HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH»`
  5. `push-per-platform` — push `«HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH»` tags to GAR
  6. `push-diags` — diagnostics artifact
+
About assembly (syft, build_info, metadata push) uses reusable {rbtgo_ark_about} scripts (`rbgja/`) embedded in the stitched JSON alongside the build steps.
+
`images:` lists `«HALLMARK»-attest-«ARCH»` tags — Cloud Build pushes each (idempotent if already present from step 5) and generates SLSA provenance.
`options.requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` ensures build fails if provenance cannot be generated.
The consumer-facing `-image` tag (produced by `buildx --push` in step 3) is platform-transparent, identical to single-arch.

[[at_workstation]]

{at_operator_s}
execute
{at_rbm_system}
commands.

[[at_base_utilities]]
//axl_voices axo_dependency
{at_base_utilities}::
The minimal set of command-line tools
(`bash`, `git`, `curl`, `openssh`, `jq`, `openssl`)
required on the
{at_workstation}.

[[at_foundry]]
{at_foundry}::
Recipe Bottle's remote build orchestration system for producing, attesting, and distributing container images via {gcb_service} and {gar_service}.
The {at_foundry} encompasses {rbtgi_depot_s}, {rbtga_vessel_s}, {rbtga_hallmark} tracking, and {rbtgr_rubric_s}.
Three vessel modes determine how images enter the {rbtgi_depot}: conjure (egress-locked build from source with SLSA provenance), bind (digest-pinned upstream mirror), and graft (local push).
Peer to {rbsc_crucible}, which handles local runtime containment.

[[at_build_service]]
{at_build_service}::
A system managing container creation through Google Cloud Build and Google Artifact Registry,
enabling
{at_operator_s}
to build containers in isolated environments with complete traceability
and cryptographic attestation.

[[at_runtime]]
{at_runtime}::
Application capable of running
{st_container_s}
on the
{at_workstation}.
Docker with Docker Compose v2 is the primary runtime for
{rbsc_crucible}
orchestration.
Podman compose support is architecturally accommodated (podman compose delegates to Docker Compose v2) but deferred.

=== Local Architecture Definitions

[[at_sessile_service]]
{at_sessile_service}::
A long-running
{rbsc_crucible}
that charges via
{rbsc_charge}
and persists until explicitly quenched.
The sessile pattern is used for services that maintain state and provide ongoing functionality.

[[at_agile_service]]
{at_agile_service}::
A transient
{rbsc_crucible}
that executes via
{rbsc_enjoin}
and terminates when its command completes.
The agile pattern is used for one-shot operations that produce output and exit.

[[rbsc_crucible]]
{rbsc_crucible}::
A complete service instance consisting of one
{at_sentry_container},
one
{at_pentacle_container},
and one
{at_bottle_container}
working together to provide secure functionality.
Orchestrated by Docker Compose with a base compose file (`rbob_compose.yml`) defining the three-container topology and health check chains, plus optional per-nameplate compose fragments for volume mounts.
Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.

[[rbsc_pentacle]]
{rbsc_pentacle}::
The privileged container within a
{rbsc_crucible}
that establishes authority over the contained network space.
Shares the
{at_sentry_image}
and configures routing so all traffic flows through the
{at_sentry_container}.
The
{at_bottle_container}
shares its network namespace via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`.

[[rbsc_sessile]]
{rbsc_sessile}::
A
{rbsc_crucible}
service mode where the bottle container runs persistently.
Charged via
{rbsc_charge}
and persists until explicitly quenched.
Used for services that maintain state and provide ongoing functionality.

[[rbsc_agile]]
{rbsc_agile}::
A
{rbsc_crucible}
service mode where transient bottles are dispatched into an already-running sentry and pentacle.
Each invocation via
{rbsc_enjoin}
creates an ephemeral bottle that terminates when its command completes.
Used for one-shot operations that produce output and exit.

[[rbsc_quench]]
{rbsc_quench}::
Tear down a
{rbsc_crucible},
removing all containers and networks for the nameplate's compose project.

[[rbsc_hail]]
{rbsc_hail}::
Diagnostic operation: open an interactive shell into the
{at_sentry_container}
of a running
{rbsc_crucible}.
Call out to the guard to inspect security enforcement state.

[[rbsc_rack]]
{rbsc_rack}::
Diagnostic operation: open an interactive shell into the
{at_bottle_container}
of a running
{rbsc_crucible}.
Compel the demon to reveal its state.

[[rbsc_scry]]
{rbsc_scry}::
Diagnostic operation: observe network traffic on a running
{rbsc_crucible}
via tcpdump.
Solomonic divination — see through the veil to the hidden topology.

[[at_sentry_container]]
{at_sentry_container}::
A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
{rbsc_crucible}.
It connects to both
{at_transit_network}
and
{at_enclave_network},
controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
The sentry discovers its network interfaces by IP at startup using `ip -o addr show to «enclave_ip»`, not by hardcoded interface name, because Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering.
Initialization and iptables configuration scripts are baked into the image at build time (zero volume mounts for security containers).

[[at_pentacle_container]]
{at_pentacle_container}::
A privileged container sharing the
{at_sentry_image}
that configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
{at_sentry_container}.
The
{at_bottle_container}
shares its network namespace via compose `network_mode: "service:pentacle"`, ensuring security policies are enforced from the first packet.
Initialization scripts are baked into the sentry image at build time (zero volume mounts for security containers).
The pentacle discovers its network interface by IP at startup using `ip -o addr show`, not by hardcoded interface name, because Docker Compose does not guarantee network-to-interface ordering.

[[at_bottle_container]]
{at_bottle_container}::
A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
{at_pentacle_container}.
It is connected only to the
{at_enclave_network}
and configured with specific
{st_volume_mount_s}.
All external communication flows through its associated
{at_sentry_container}.

[[at_enclave_network]]
{at_enclave_network}::
An isolated network connecting a
{at_bottle_container}
to its
{at_sentry_container},
using
{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
for addressing.
This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
{at_bottle_container}.

[[at_transit_network]]
{at_transit_network}::
The network interface that connects a
{at_sentry_container}
to the
{at_workstation_p}
network resources.
This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.

[[at_nameplate]]
{at_nameplate}::
A configuration file that defines all aspects of a
{rbsc_crucible},
including its images, network settings, and security policies.
Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
{at_rbm_system}
can instantiate.

// [[at_ebpf_program]] removed — historical (Podman era), superseded by compose network_mode

[[st_container]]
{st_container}::
A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.

[[st_volume_mount]]
{st_volume_mount}::
A configuration that allows
{st_container_s}
to access specified portions of the host filesystem.

[[st_port_map]]
{st_port_map}::
A specification of how network ports should be exposed and mapped between
{st_container_s}
and the host system.

[[at_sentry_image]]
{at_sentry_image}::
A
{st_image}
that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
{at_sentry_container}.

[[at_moniker]]
{at_moniker}::
A unique identifier that distinguishes each
{rbsc_crucible}
instance within the
{at_rbm_system}.
This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.

[[at_rbm_console]]
{at_rbm_console}::
The primary operational automation that implements the
{at_rbm_system}
lifecycle rules for managing
{rbsc_crucible_s}.

[[at_rbm_config]]
{at_rbm_config}::
Configuration settings for the
{at_rbm_system},
including
{at_rbm_secret_s}
and environment-specific parameters.
This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.

[[at_rbm_secret]]
{at_rbm_secret}::
A sensitive configuration value needed by the
{at_rbm_system},
such as registry credentials.

[[at_rbm_repo]]
{at_rbm_repo}::
The version control repository containing the
{at_rbm_system}
implementation, including operational automation,

[[at_user_repo]]
{at_user_repo}::
A version control repository owned by an

where the

is instantiated.

[[at_container_registry]]
{at_container_registry}::
A service that stores and provides access to
{st_published_image_s}.

[[at_startup_script]]
{at_startup_script}::
A shell script baked into a vessel image at build time that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
{rbsc_crucible}.
The sentry startup script (`rbjs_sentry.sh`) configures iptables and dnsmasq; the pentacle init script (`rbjp_pentacle.sh`) configures routing.
Both live in the sentry vessel's build context directory and are COPYed into the image during conjure.

[[at_transit_network_name]]
{at_transit_network_name}::
The compose `default` network for the service's compose project.
Compose names it `${RBRN_MONIKER}_default`.

[[at_bottle_container_name]]

[[at_sentry_container_name]]

The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
{rbrn_moniker}.

[[st_dockerfile]]
{st_dockerfile}::
A specification file that defines how to build a
{st_image}.

[[st_published_image]]

{st_image}
that has been uploaded to a
{at_container_registry}.

[[st_image]]

A template used to create
{st_container}
instances, containing the service's code, runtime, and dependencies.

[[st_image_store]]
{st_image_store}::
The local storage location for
{st_image_s}
on the
{at_operator_p}
{at_workstation}.

[[st_subnet]]

A logically visible subdivision of IP network address space.

[[st_gateway]]

Tabtarget colophon for compact hallmark plumb.
Displays trust posture summary: vessel type, source, SLSA provenance, base image, build output, cache delta, SBOM summary, and vouch results.

[[rbrr_gcb_machine_type]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}::
Compute Engine machine type for the private worker pool, consumed at pool creation time by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}.
Default: `e2-standard-2`.
The pool resource holds this value; individual build requests do not specify machine type.

[[rbrr_gcb_pool_stem]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_string
{rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}::
Worker pool base name (suffixed with -tether/-airgap by derived constants).
Derived constants `RBDC_POOL_TETHER` and `RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP` expand this stem into full resource paths.
Must be set to the pool base name created by
{rbtgo_depot_levy}
for this depot.
Builds always use a private pool; the default shared pool is not supported.
`options.machineType` is omitted from the build JSON — machine type is a property of the pool resource.

NOTE: *Private pool is always required.*
Private pools are a prerequisite for VPC Service Controls and `NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS` hardening tiers (see `RBSCB-CloudBuildRoadmap.adoc` tier definitions).
Pricing is region-dependent: in us-central1/us-east4, private pool e2-standard-2 matches the default pool rate ($0.006/build-minute); in other regions the private pool rate is up to 1.4× higher.
This cost is accepted because the security prerequisite is non-negotiable and maintaining two code paths (conditional pool vs. default) doubles configuration surface.
+
Cloud Build supports two distinct machine type naming systems:
the default shared pool uses Cloud Build enum values (`E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `N1_HIGHCPU_32`) in `options.machineType` of the build JSON;
private pools use Compute Engine instance type names (`e2-standard-2`, `n2d-standard-4`, `c3-standard-8`) in `workerConfig.machineType` at pool creation.
Valid private pool families: `e2`, `n2d`, `c3` (availability varies by region).
Since Recipe Bottle uses only private pools, only the Compute Engine format is valid for
{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}.

[[rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_integer
{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}::
Minimum concurrent Cloud Build execution count.

[[rbrr_gcb_timeout]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_duration
{rbrr_gcb_timeout}::
Maximum duration for Cloud Build operations.

[[rbrr_public_docs_url]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_url
{rbrr_public_docs_url}::
Public docs URL — readme target for buh_tlt glossary links, updated per-release or per-incorporation.

=== Vessel Regime (RBRV)

[[rbrv_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_manifold
{rbrv_regime}::
Configuration regime for container image build specifications.

[[rbrv_sigil]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrv_sigil}::
Vessel identifier for
{rbrv_regime};
basis for directory and ark naming.

[[rbrv_description]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_description
{rbrv_description}::
Human-readable documentation for
{rbrv_regime}.

[[rbrv_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_username
{rbrv_user}::
Runtime user for
{rbrv_regime}
containers.
When set, downstream consumers (compose, SSH, rack) use this as the container user identity.
When unset, the container uses its image default (typically root).
Applies across all vessel modes — conjure vessels create the user in their Dockerfile, bind and graft vessels declare a user that already exists in the image.

[[rbrv_vessel_mode]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axt_enumeration
{rbrv_vessel_mode}::
Build mode for
{rbrv_regime}.
Determines which variable group is active.

[[rbrv_vessel_mode_bind]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}::
Selects binding configuration: vessel uses an existing upstream image.

[[rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}::
Selects conjuring configuration: vessel is built from a Dockerfile.

[[rbrv_vessel_mode_graft]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}::
Selects grafting configuration: vessel accepts a locally-built image pushed to GAR.
The name comes from botanical grafting — foreign material spliced onto rootstock.
The graft may take or may not; the metaphor fits the trust posture exactly.

[[rbrv_group_binding]]
//axvr_group axd_conditional
{rbrv_group_binding}::
Variables for binding vessels within
{rbrv_regime}.

[[rbrv_group_conjuring]]
//axvr_group axd_conditional
{rbrv_group_conjuring}::
Variables for conjuring vessels within
{rbrv_regime}.

[[rbrv_bind_image]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_bind_image}::
Container image reference for
{rbrv_regime}
binding vessels.

[[rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_path
{rbrv_bind_optional_dockerfile}::
Optional path to reference Dockerfile for the upstream image in
{rbrv_regime}
binding vessels.
When configured, the Dockerfile content is embedded in the
{rbtga_ark_about}
artifact as `recipe.txt`.

[[rbrv_conjure_dockerfile]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_path
{rbrv_conjure_dockerfile}::
Dockerfile path for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.

[[rbrv_conjure_bldcontext]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_path
{rbrv_conjure_bldcontext}::
Build context directory for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring.

[[rbrv_conjure_platforms]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_platform_list
{rbrv_conjure_platforms}::
Target platform architectures for
{rbrv_regime}
multi-platform builds.

[[rbrv_group_image]]
//axvr_group axd_optional
{rbrv_group_image}::
Variables declaring base image dependencies within
{rbrv_regime}.
Up to 3 indexed slots (n = 1, 2, 3) for multi-stage Dockerfile support.
Applies to
{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
vessels.

[[rbrv_image_origin]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_image_origin}::
Upstream base image tag declaration for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
Vessel author declares intent (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`).
The Dockerfile uses `ARG RBF_IMAGE_n` / `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_n}` -- the Foundry substitutes the resolved reference via `--build-arg` at conjure time.

[[rbrv_image_anchor]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_image_anchor}::
GAR-mirrored, content-addressed reference for a base image in
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
Written by
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
Format: `«sanitized-origin»-«10-char-sha256»` (e.g., `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`).
The anchor serves as both the GAR image tag and the regime variable value.
Enshrined images are stored in the shared `enshrine` namespace (`«repo»/enshrine:«anchor»`), not per-vessel — vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined artifact.
When present, conjure resolves the base image from GAR via this anchor.
When absent, conjure passes
{rbrv_image_origin}
through as the upstream tag (development path).

[[rbrv_group_grafting]]
//axvr_group axd_conditional
{rbrv_group_grafting}::
Variables for grafting vessels within
{rbrv_regime}.

[[rbrv_graft_image]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_graft_image}::
Default local image reference for
{rbrv_regime}
grafting vessels.
Optional; when empty, the image reference must be provided as a runtime parameter to
{rbtgo_ark_graft}.

[[rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_path
{rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile}::
Optional path to Dockerfile used for the local build in
{rbrv_regime}
grafting vessels.
When configured, the Dockerfile content is embedded in the
{rbtga_ark_about}
artifact as `recipe.txt`.

include::RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc[]

[[rbrn_bottle_vessel]]

[[rbrn_bottle_hallmark]]

[[rbrn_entry_mode]]

Mode for user-initiated entry functionality.

[[rbrn_entry_mode_disabled]]

Entry functionality is disabled; no user-initiated connections.

[[rbrn_entry_mode_enabled]]

{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}::
Entry functionality is enabled; user-initiated connections accepted.

[[rbrn_entry_port_workstation]]

External port exposed on the transit network for user entry.

[[rbrn_entry_port_enclave]]

{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}::
Port used between sentry and bottle containers for entry traffic.

[[rbrn_enclave_base_ip]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_ipv4
{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}::
Base IPv4 address defining the enclave network range.

[[rbrn_enclave_netmask]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_netmask
{rbrn_enclave_netmask}::
Network mask width for enclave network addressing; 8-30.

[[rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_ipv4

[[rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_ipv4
{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}::
IP address assigned to the bottle container within the enclave.

[[rbrn_uplink_port_min]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_port
{rbrn_uplink_port_min}::
Minimum port number for bottle-initiated outbound connections.

[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axt_enumeration
{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}::
DNS resolution mode for bottle-initiated requests.

[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_disabled]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_disabled}::
DNS resolution disabled; no bottle-initiated DNS queries.

[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_global]]
